fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 is inverted. Clamping is applied when `clamp` is `False` and skipped when `clamp` is `True`, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: (clamp and ((y__3 >= y0 and y__3 <= y1) or (y__3 >= y1 and y__3 <= y0))) or (not clamp and y__3 == y__1)

constraints.intent: After the conditional block, the value of y must be consistent with the clamping setting.
If clamping is enabled but the clamping logic is skipped, the value must already be within the valid y-range.
The clamping logic branch should only be executed when clamping is enabled.
The bug only manifests during extrapolation, when t is outside the [0, 1] range.
